stl:listening:1:comprehending-informatio.description: Listen and get a few obvious details from someone being introduced (such as where they are from). Listen to a medical receptionist’s confirmation of personal information (such as name, address and phone number). Listen to a request for the time and a response that gives the time.
stl:listening:1:comprehending-informatio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:1:comprehending-informatio.taskLabelText: Listen and get a few obvious details from someone being introduced (such as where they are from). Listen to a medical receptionist’s confirmation of personal information (such as name, address and phone number). Listen to a request for the time and a response that gives the time.
stl:listening:1:comprehending-informatio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 1 - Comprehending Information

stl:listening:1:comprehending-instructio.description: Follow instructions, directions and requests from a teacher, receptionist or sales representative. (How do you spell your name? Over there. Sign on the line. Initial here. Go upstairs. Can I see some identification?)
stl:listening:1:comprehending-instructio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:1:comprehending-instructio.taskLabelText: Follow instructions, directions and requests from a teacher, receptionist or sales representative. (How do you spell your name? Over there. Sign on the line. Initial here. Go upstairs. Can I see some identification?)
stl:listening:1:comprehending-instructio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 1 - Comprehending Instructions

stl:listening:1:getting-things-done:001.description: Listen and respond to common expressions used in a store (such as a sales clerk’s offer to provide assistance: May I help you?). Listen and respond to expressions used to attract attention and request assistance. (Excuse me. Hello. Can you help me? Pardon me.)
stl:listening:1:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:1:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Listen and respond to common expressions used in a store (such as a sales clerk’s offer to provide assistance: May I help you?). Listen and respond to expressions used to attract attention and request assistance. (Excuse me. Hello. Can you help me? Pardon me.)
stl:listening:1:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 1 - Getting Things Done

stl:listening:1:interacting-with-others:.description: Listen and respond to a short greeting that includes a goodwill expression from a neighbour, co- worker or classmate. (How’s it going? Great. Good to see you. How's your daughter? Fine, thank you. Talk to you later. Have a good day.) In a social interaction with an acquaintance, recognize and respond to an appeal for repetition or clarification. Listen and respond to simple expressions of welcome made by a neighbour or teacher. (Please come in. Please sit down.)
stl:listening:1:interacting-with-others:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:1:interacting-with-others:.taskLabelText: Listen and respond to a short greeting that includes a goodwill expression from a neighbour, co- worker or classmate. (How’s it going? Great. Good to see you. How's your daughter? Fine, thank you. Talk to you later. Have a good day.) In a social interaction with an acquaintance, recognize and respond to an appeal for repetition or clarification. Listen and respond to simple expressions of welcome made by a neighbour or teacher. (Please come in. Please sit down.)
stl:listening:1:interacting-with-others:.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 1 - Interacting With Others

stl:listening:2:comprehending-informatio.description: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate give basic personal information about his/her life. Listen to a classmate give simple personal details, such as spelling his/her name and giving a phone number and email address. Listen to a client giving his/her license plate number and the name and title of a person he/she is meeting at a workplace reception desk.
stl:listening:2:comprehending-informatio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:2:comprehending-informatio.taskLabelText: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate give basic personal information about his/her life. Listen to a classmate give simple personal details, such as spelling his/her name and giving a phone number and email address. Listen to a client giving his/her license plate number and the name and title of a person he/she is meeting at a workplace reception desk.
stl:listening:2:comprehending-informatio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 2 - Comprehending Information

stl:listening:2:comprehending-instructio.description: Follow simple instructions from a salesperson in order to locate items in various sections of a store. (The men’s section is upstairs, to the left.) Follow instructions for completing a class assignment. (Put your name at the top of the page. Read each question carefully. Answer true or false. Circle the right answers.) Follow oral directions from a GPS device about how to reach a location. (Turn right in 50 meters.)
stl:listening:2:comprehending-instructio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:2:comprehending-instructio.taskLabelText: Follow simple instructions from a salesperson in order to locate items in various sections of a store. (The men’s section is upstairs, to the left.) Follow instructions for completing a class assignment. (Put your name at the top of the page. Read each question carefully. Answer true or false. Circle the right answers.) Follow oral directions from a GPS device about how to reach a location. (Turn right in 50 meters.)
stl:listening:2:comprehending-instructio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 2 - Comprehending Instructions

stl:listening:2:getting-things-done:001.description: Listen to customers making simple food orders in a restaurant. Listen to and respond to a request to borrow office supplies or use a computer. Listen and respond to a supervisor’s request to work overtime. Listen to a teacher’s request or warning during a fire drill (Leave your belongings. Close the door behind you.) and respond accordingly.
stl:listening:2:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:2:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Listen to customers making simple food orders in a restaurant. Listen to and respond to a request to borrow office supplies or use a computer. Listen and respond to a supervisor’s request to work overtime. Listen to a teacher’s request or warning during a fire drill (Leave your belongings. Close the door behind you.) and respond accordingly.
stl:listening:2:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 2 - Getting Things Done

stl:listening:2:interacting-with-others:.description: Listen and respond to an informal greeting, simple exchange and leave-taking when meeting a child’s teacher, a new colleague or a neighbour. Listen and respond to a formal introduction by a teacher or supervisor. Listen and respond to an apology from a classmate or co-worker for mistakenly taking a pen or pencil.
stl:listening:2:interacting-with-others:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:2:interacting-with-others:.taskLabelText: Listen and respond to an informal greeting, simple exchange and leave-taking when meeting a child’s teacher, a new colleague or a neighbour. Listen and respond to a formal introduction by a teacher or supervisor. Listen and respond to an apology from a classmate or co-worker for mistakenly taking a pen or pencil.
stl:listening:2:interacting-with-others:.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 2 - Interacting With Others

stl:listening:3:comprehending-informatio.description: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate’s story about a personal experience. Listen to descriptions of various people to identify them in a photograph. Listen to a short, simple announcement at school to inform a classmate, who is absent, of what action to take (e.g. bring food to share, arrange to stay late).
stl:listening:3:comprehending-informatio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:3:comprehending-informatio.taskLabelText: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate’s story about a personal experience. Listen to descriptions of various people to identify them in a photograph. Listen to a short, simple announcement at school to inform a classmate, who is absent, of what action to take (e.g. bring food to share, arrange to stay late).
stl:listening:3:comprehending-informatio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 3 - Comprehending Information

stl:listening:3:comprehending-instructio.description: Follow simple instructions from a doctor to deal with a common ailment (such as the flu or a cold). Follow instructions for a simple recipe to identify the order of the steps. Follow directions to locate items in a room, on a map or in a diagram. (Can you get me the book? It’s in the middle cabinet, on the top shelf, on the right-hand side.) Follow instructions for a basic task at work, such as making a photocopy or cleaning a piece of equipment.
stl:listening:3:comprehending-instructio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:3:comprehending-instructio.taskLabelText: Follow simple instructions from a doctor to deal with a common ailment (such as the flu or a cold). Follow instructions for a simple recipe to identify the order of the steps. Follow directions to locate items in a room, on a map or in a diagram. (Can you get me the book? It’s in the middle cabinet, on the top shelf, on the right-hand side.) Follow instructions for a basic task at work, such as making a photocopy or cleaning a piece of equipment.
stl:listening:3:comprehending-instructio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 3 - Comprehending Instructions

stl:listening:3:getting-things-done:001.description: Listen to a colleague’s request for a day off to determine the nature of the request and the reason. (Can I take Friday off? My son is sick. He has a cold.) Listen to a teacher giving permission for a student to borrow a book. (Can I borrow that book? Sure. It's really interesting. I hope you like it. Please return it in 2 weeks.)
stl:listening:3:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:3:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Listen to a colleague’s request for a day off to determine the nature of the request and the reason. (Can I take Friday off? My son is sick. He has a cold.) Listen to a teacher giving permission for a student to borrow a book. (Can I borrow that book? Sure. It's really interesting. I hope you like it. Please return it in 2 weeks.)
stl:listening:3:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 3 - Getting Things Done

stl:listening:3:interacting-with-others:.description: Listen and respond to a friend’s simple, casual, familiar small talk. Listen to a friend or co-worker describing plans for the weekend. Listen to a discussion about household tasks to determine who will do what.
stl:listening:3:interacting-with-others:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:3:interacting-with-others:.taskLabelText: Listen and respond to a friend’s simple, casual, familiar small talk. Listen to a friend or co-worker describing plans for the weekend. Listen to a discussion about household tasks to determine who will do what.
stl:listening:3:interacting-with-others:.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 3 - Interacting With Others

stl:listening:4:comprehending-informatio.description: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate’s story about shopping at a particular store to decide whether or not to shop there. Listen to a very short, clear weather report to use the information to decide what to wear. Listen to a brief description of topics to be covered in a course.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-informatio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-informatio.taskLabelText: Listen to a friend, colleague or classmate’s story about shopping at a particular store to decide whether or not to shop there. Listen to a very short, clear weather report to use the information to decide what to wear. Listen to a brief description of topics to be covered in a course.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-informatio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 4 - Comprehending Information

stl:listening:4:comprehending-instructio.description: Follow instructions on safety or security procedures at work. Follow directions to locate an office, school or tourist attraction on a map. Follow directions on using public transit given over the phone. Follow a teacher’s instructions for performing a mathematical calculation. Follow a pharmacist’s instructions on how to take a specific medication.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-instructio.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-instructio.taskLabelText: Follow instructions on safety or security procedures at work. Follow directions to locate an office, school or tourist attraction on a map. Follow directions on using public transit given over the phone. Follow a teacher’s instructions for performing a mathematical calculation. Follow a pharmacist’s instructions on how to take a specific medication.
stl:listening:4:comprehending-instructio.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 4 - Comprehending Instructions

stl:listening:4:getting-things-done:001.description: Listen to a short, simple commercial about a product or service to get the gist and enough information to decide whether the product is worth purchasing. Listen to an airport ticket agent telling a traveller that a boarding pass cannot be given because the flight is now full and explaining when the next available flight will be departing. Listen to an exchange between a sales clerk and a customer to determine the nature of the transaction.
stl:listening:4:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:listening:4:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Listen to a short, simple commercial about a product or service to get the gist and enough information to decide whether the product is worth purchasing. Listen to an airport ticket agent telling a traveller that a boarding pass cannot be given because the flight is now full and explaining when the next available flight will be departing. Listen to an exchange between a sales clerk and a customer to determine the nature of the transaction.
stl:listening:4:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 4 - Getting Things Done

stl:listening:4:interacting-with-others:.description: Listen to a co-worker, friend or classmate's likes and dislikes regarding the organization of an upcoming social event to respond and contribute ideas. Listen to a phone message from a medical receptionist changing the date and time of an appointment. Listen to an exchange between co-workers talking about their preferences for various work shifts and the reasons for their preferences. Listen to compliments to determine whether they are sincere.
stl:listening:4:interacting-with-others:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

Listen to an exchange between co-workers talking about their preferences for various work shifts and the reasons for their preferences. Listen to compliments to determine whether they are sincere.
stl:listening:4:interacting-with-others:.title: Sample Task - Listening CLB 4 - Interacting With Others

stl:reading:1:comprehending-information:.description: Refer to a picture dictionary to understand a short shopping list of common daily items. Get the gist of an event by reading captions associated with photographs, such as for a child’s birthday party, a wedding or a retirement party.
stl:reading:1:comprehending-information:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:reading:1:comprehending-instructions.description: Read and follow onscreen instructions for withdrawing money from an automatic teller machine (ATM). Read and follow simple instructions to complete a class or work assignment. (Type the form. Make 12 copies. Sign the copies.)
stl:reading:1:comprehending-instructions.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:reading:1:comprehending-instructions.taskLabelText: Read and follow onscreen instructions for withdrawing money from an automatic teller machine (ATM). Read and follow simple instructions to complete a class or work assignment. (Type the form. Make 12 copies. Sign the copies.)

stl:reading:1:getting-things-done:001.description: Identify where to write personal information in the name and address section of a very simple application form for an English class. Identify prices, sub-totals, taxes and total on a sales receipt. Locate familiar words on a simple map to find a street. Identify dates and amounts on a pay stub. Check expiration dates on foods and drugs in order to decide whether to keep them or throw them away. Read a simple common sign to heed a rule or warning. (Stop. No parking. No smoking.)
stl:reading:1:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

Check expiration dates on foods and drugs in order to decide whether to keep them or throw them away. Read a simple common sign to heed a rule or warning. (Stop. No parking. No smoking.)
stl:reading:1:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Reading CLB 1 - Getting Things Done

stl:reading:1:interacting-with-others:00.description: Read a short personal message (such as a postcard, farewell message or invitation) from a friend, classmate or colleague. Read a short email or text message invitation to lunch or a party from a friend, co-worker or classmate to find when and where the function is.
stl:reading:1:interacting-with-others:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:reading:2:comprehending-information:.description: Match a shopping list with pictures in a catalogue or with the actual items. Read a few short, simple sentences about a familiar person and use key words to identify a few concrete facts. Read short sentences about own occupation and identify a few basic, familiar, concrete facts.
stl:reading:2:comprehending-information:.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:reading:2:comprehending-instructions.description: Read directions in point form to get to a nearby location. Read a legibly written note from a neighbour with simple instructions on how to take care of their home or pet while they are away. Read and follow short, point-form instructions from a supervisor about a simple work-related task.

stl:reading:2:comprehending-instructions.title: Sample Task - Reading CLB 2 - Comprehending Instructions

stl:reading:2:getting-things-done:001.description: Read the hours of operation on a store sign in order to plan a shopping trip. Get basic information about specials from a grocery store flyer. Identify details on a monthly credit card or utility statement to make a payment or check for accuracy. Find details on an invoice or bill to pay the correct amount. Refer to a bus schedule to find out when the next bus will arrive.
stl:reading:2:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:reading:3:comprehending-information:.description: Read the first paragraph of a simple story to determine what it is about. Use an online bilingual dictionary to find the meanings and pronunciations of words and phrases related to own occupation or area of interest.

stl:reading:4:comprehending-information:.description: Read a short news article (print or online) to get the details. Use a simple statistical table (print or online) listing place of birth and number of people in Canada to identify where most immigrants come from. Read a simple description of an event or phenomenon and locate a few concrete facts. Read a simple description about a company and locate a few concrete facts (when it started, what it does, how many people it employs). Use an online encyclopedia to find relevant information for a class project. Read simple advertisements of 2 products to decide which product to buy.

stl:speaking:1:getting-things-done:001.description: Attract a server’s attention and make a request. (Excuse me. Coffee, please.) Ask for the time. Describe amounts of money. (I have 2 quarters. Here is 1 dime. I have 60 cents.) Ask for assistance (Help me, please.)
stl:speaking:1:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:1:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Attract a server’s attention and make a request. (Excuse me. Coffee, please.) Ask for the time. Describe amounts of money. (I have 2 quarters. Here is 1 dime. I have 60 cents.) Ask for assistance (Help me, please.)
stl:speaking:1:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 1 - Getting Things Done

stl:speaking:1:giving-instructions:001.description: Give polite positive or negative instructions or commands to a friend, co-worker, sales clerk or classmate. (Water the plants, ok? Come to my house. Please don’t do that!)
stl:speaking:1:giving-instructions:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:1:giving-instructions:001.taskLabelText: Give polite positive or negative instructions or commands to a friend, co-worker, sales clerk or classmate. (Water the plants, ok? Come to my house. Please don’t do that!)
stl:speaking:1:giving-instructions:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 1 - Giving Instructions

stl:speaking:1:interacting-with-others:0.description: Introduce self and respond to another person’s introduction. Initiate a very short conversation with a classmate or neighbour. Indicate non-understanding if necessary. (I don’t understand. My English is not good. Speak slowly, please.) Respond to a common goodwill message with a simple and appropriate stock phrase. (Have a good weekend. Thanks. You too.)
stl:speaking:1:interacting-with-others:0.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:1:interacting-with-others:0.taskLabelText: Introduce self and respond to another person’s introduction. Initiate a very short conversation with a classmate or neighbour. Indicate non-understanding if necessary. (I don’t understand. My English is not good. Speak slowly, please.) Respond to a common goodwill message with a simple and appropriate stock phrase. (Have a good weekend. Thanks. You too.)
stl:speaking:1:interacting-with-others:0.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 1 - Interacting With Others

stl:speaking:1:sharing-information:001.description: Answer 2 or 3 questions from a co-worker, classmate or neighbour about self and family. Answer 2 or 3 personal information questions (such as name, address, and phone number) from a librarian or store clerk.
stl:speaking:1:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:1:sharing-information:001.taskLabelText: Answer 2 or 3 questions from a co-worker, classmate or neighbour about self and family. Answer 2 or 3 personal information questions (such as name, address, and phone number) from a librarian or store clerk.
stl:speaking:1:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 1 - Sharing Information

stl:speaking:2:getting-things-done:001.description: Make a simple food order in a restaurant or cafeteria at work. (Coffee with milk and sugar, please.) Request an item from a friend, co-worker, or classmate. Warn a friend about an icy sidewalk. (Be careful. Don’t fall.) Request that the landlord fix a simple problem in an apartment. (Please fix my sink.) Respond to someone who is bothersome. (Please leave me alone.)
stl:speaking:2:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:2:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Make a simple food order in a restaurant or cafeteria at work. (Coffee with milk and sugar, please.) Request an item from a friend, co-worker, or classmate. Warn a friend about an icy sidewalk. (Be careful. Don’t fall.) Request that the landlord fix a simple problem in an apartment. (Please fix my sink.) Respond to someone who is bothersome. (Please leave me alone.)
stl:speaking:2:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 2 - Getting Things Done

stl:speaking:2:giving-instructions:001.description: Give short directions to a friend, co-worker, or classmate to a location in a building. (Go down the hall. Turn right. Take the elevator. Go to the main office. Get a form.) Give a short instruction to a friend to help him/her find something. (It’s in the closet. Look under the chair.)
stl:speaking:2:giving-instructions:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:2:giving-instructions:001.taskLabelText: Give short directions to a friend, co-worker, or classmate to a location in a building. (Go down the hall. Turn right. Take the elevator. Go to the main office. Get a form.) Give a short instruction to a friend to help him/her find something. (It’s in the closet. Look under the chair.)
stl:speaking:2:giving-instructions:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 2 - Giving Instructions

stl:speaking:2:interacting-with-others:0.description: Participate in a short, casual conversation. Introduce self and ask about the other person at a party, in the workplace, or in class. End the conversation appropriately. At a doctor’s office, greet the receptionist and exchange a few simple pleasantries. (Hello. How are you? Nice to see you. It’s a warm day.) State the reason for the visit and provide personal information, such as a health card number.
stl:speaking:2:interacting-with-others:0.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:2:interacting-with-others:0.taskLabelText: Participate in a short, casual conversation. Introduce self and ask about the other person at a party, in the workplace, or in class. End the conversation appropriately. At a doctor’s office, greet the receptionist and exchange a few simple pleasantries. (Hello. How are you? Nice to see you. It’s a warm day.) State the reason for the visit and provide personal information, such as a health card number.
stl:speaking:2:interacting-with-others:0.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 2 - Interacting With Others

stl:speaking:2:sharing-information:001.description: Give basic personal information to a teacher or daycare worker. Describe a personal photograph of a family member or a friend in a few short phrases. Ask questions about a classmate’s photograph. Talk with a friend about a favourite food or possession. Ask questions about the friend’s favourite food or possession. Give a brief concrete description of basic abilities related to work. (I can type. I can use a computer.)
stl:speaking:2:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:2:sharing-information:001.taskLabelText: Give basic personal information to a teacher or daycare worker. Describe a personal photograph of a family member or a friend in a few short phrases. Ask questions about a classmate’s photograph. Talk with a friend about a favourite food or possession. Ask questions about the friend’s favourite food or possession. Give a brief concrete description of basic abilities related to work. (I can type. I can use a computer.)
stl:speaking:2:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 2 - Sharing Information

stl:speaking:3:getting-things-done:001.description: Ask an employer or teacher for permission to do something. (May I leave early today? Can I write the test tomorrow?) Ask a friend to help move, organize a party, or house-sit. (Can you help me move? I really appreciate it. Will you water my plants? Can you do it?) Advise someone of a dangerous situation. (Watch your step! Be careful. The floor is wet.)
stl:speaking:3:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:3:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Ask an employer or teacher for permission to do something. (May I leave early today? Can I write the test tomorrow?) Ask a friend to help move, organize a party, or house-sit. (Can you help me move? I really appreciate it. Will you water my plants? Can you do it?) Advise someone of a dangerous situation. (Watch your step! Be careful. The floor is wet.)
stl:speaking:3:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 3 - Getting Things Done

stl:speaking:3:giving-instructions:001.description: Give a short set of instructions on how to set an alarm clock, use a long- distance calling card, or print a file from a computer. Give directions to a co-worker or classmate on how to get to the lunchroom.
stl:speaking:3:giving-instructions:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:3:giving-instructions:001.taskLabelText: Give a short set of instructions on how to set an alarm clock, use a long- distance calling card, or print a file from a computer. Give directions to a co-worker or classmate on how to get to the lunchroom.
stl:speaking:3:giving-instructions:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 3 - Giving Instructions

stl:speaking:3:interacting-with-others:0.description: Greet a neighbour, ask about his/her health, and talk about the weather. Extend the conversation by asking questions. Introduce a teacher or employer to a family member. Leave a simple voice mail message for a friend, co-worker, or classmate. (Hello, this is Maria. Please call me back. My number is…)
stl:speaking:3:interacting-with-others:0.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:3:interacting-with-others:0.taskLabelText: Greet a neighbour, ask about his/her health, and talk about the weather. Extend the conversation by asking questions. Introduce a teacher or employer to a family member. Leave a simple voice mail message for a friend, co-worker, or classmate. (Hello, this is Maria. Please call me back. My number is…)
stl:speaking:3:interacting-with-others:0.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 3 - Interacting With Others

stl:speaking:3:sharing-information:001.description: Give a brief simple description of a home or work space. Give a brief basic description of health or feelings to a doctor or nurse. Talk about simple activities of last weekend. Ask about a friend’s weekend. Describe simple plans or intentions for a future event or activity.
stl:speaking:3:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:3:sharing-information:001.taskLabelText: Give a brief simple description of a home or work space. Give a brief basic description of health or feelings to a doctor or nurse. Talk about simple activities of last weekend. Ask about a friend’s weekend. Describe simple plans or intentions for a future event or activity.
stl:speaking:3:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 3 - Sharing Information

stl:speaking:4:getting-things-done:001.description: Request a room in a hotel and ask about services. (Is there parking? Is there a pool and an exercise room?) Respond to a warning about a simple bylaw violation. (Oh, I didn’t see the sign. Thanks for telling me.) Request a review of a paycheque because overtime hours were not included. Respond to an instructor’s warning about handing in an assignment late. (I have a family emergency. Can I bring it next week?)
stl:speaking:4:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:4:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Request a room in a hotel and ask about services. (Is there parking? Is there a pool and an exercise room?) Respond to a warning about a simple bylaw violation. (Oh, I didn’t see the sign. Thanks for telling me.) Request a review of a paycheque because overtime hours were not included. Respond to an instructor’s warning about handing in an assignment late. (I have a family emergency. Can I bring it next week?)
stl:speaking:4:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 4 - Getting Things Done

stl:speaking:4:giving-instructions:001.description: Give directions to a home or business. Give instructions to a new co-worker on how to use an appliance, machine, or system at work, such as a photocopier or coffee-maker, or how to transfer a call on a telephone system. Instruct a classmate on how to register for a course.
stl:speaking:4:giving-instructions:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:4:giving-instructions:001.taskLabelText: Give directions to a home or business. Give instructions to a new co-worker on how to use an appliance, machine, or system at work, such as a photocopier or coffee-maker, or how to transfer a call on a telephone system. Instruct a classmate on how to register for a course.
stl:speaking:4:giving-instructions:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 4 - Giving Instructions

stl:speaking:4:interacting-with-others:0.description: Initiate a short, informal conversation with a friend, co-worker or classmate. Engage in small talk, compliment the person and/or respond to a compliment. End the conversation appropriately. Invite a friend, co-worker, or classmate to lunch. Answer the phone at work. Provide information to the caller (such as company name, hours of operation, basic information about a product or service).
stl:speaking:4:interacting-with-others:0.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:4:interacting-with-others:0.taskLabelText: Initiate a short, informal conversation with a friend, co-worker or classmate. Engage in small talk, compliment the person and/or respond to a compliment. End the conversation appropriately. Invite a friend, co-worker, or classmate to lunch. Answer the phone at work. Provide information to the caller (such as company name, hours of operation, basic information about a product or service).
stl:speaking:4:interacting-with-others:0.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 4 - Interacting With Others

stl:speaking:4:sharing-information:001.description: Tell a short story about a happy day. Tell a short story about a common activity (such as finding a job or an apartment). Give a brief description of a typical day at home or at work. Tell what happened (by narrating a brief series of events) in a short TV show.
stl:speaking:4:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:speaking:4:sharing-information:001.taskLabelText: Tell a short story about a happy day. Tell a short story about a common activity (such as finding a job or an apartment). Give a brief description of a typical day at home or at work. Tell what happened (by narrating a brief series of events) in a short TV show.
stl:speaking:4:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Speaking CLB 4 - Sharing Information

stl:speaking:5:sharing-information:001.description: Starter sample-task-label scaffold placeholder. Replace with canonical sample task labels from mapped fragments.
stl:speaking:5:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Seed rule: create one starter sample-task-label per competency seed until direct mapped sample-task fragments are available.
stl:speaking:5:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task Seed - Speaking CLB 5 - Speaking CLB 5 - Sharing Information

stl:writing:1:getting-things-done:001.description: Fill out the personal identification area of a simple change-of-address form with a few details (such as date, first and last name, address, postal code, phone number, and date of birth). Fill out the personal identification area of an application to join a language class or apply for a job (with assistance from an employer, administrative assistant, or instructor).
stl:writing:1:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:1:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Fill out the personal identification area of a simple change-of-address form with a few details (such as date, first and last name, address, postal code, phone number, and date of birth). Fill out the personal identification area of an application to join a language class or apply for a job (with assistance from an employer, administrative assistant, or instructor).
stl:writing:1:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 1 - Getting Things Done

stl:writing:1:interacting-with-others:00.description: Complete a short message with appropriate phrases to thank a classmate, congratulate a co-worker, or wish a friend well (with assistance from another person or by referring to an example message). Write a 1-clause message to comment on a friend’s newly posted picture on a social networking site.
stl:writing:1:interacting-with-others:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:1:interacting-with-others:00.taskLabelText: Complete a short message with appropriate phrases to thank a classmate, congratulate a co-worker, or wish a friend well (with assistance from another person or by referring to an example message). Write a 1-clause message to comment on a friend’s newly posted picture on a social networking site.
stl:writing:1:interacting-with-others:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 1 - Interacting With Others

stl:writing:1:reproducing-information:00.description: Copy names, addresses, and phone numbers from a telephone directory (either print or online). Copy selected information from signs and simple schedules (such as hours of operation of a store, or a work schedule) for personal use. Copy the company name, address, application deadline, and contact information from a simple job ad. Copy emergency fire instructions posted in a building.
stl:writing:1:reproducing-information:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:1:reproducing-information:00.taskLabelText: Copy names, addresses, and phone numbers from a telephone directory (either print or online). Copy selected information from signs and simple schedules (such as hours of operation of a store, or a work schedule) for personal use. Copy the company name, address, application deadline, and contact information from a simple job ad. Copy emergency fire instructions posted in a building.
stl:writing:1:reproducing-information:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 1 - Reproducing Information

stl:writing:1:sharing-information:001.description: In a language class, complete a simple guided writing text about self by filling in blanks. (My name is _____. I am _______. I am from _______. I have _______.) Write answers to simple questions about immediate needs with assistance from a family member or settlement worker.
stl:writing:1:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:1:sharing-information:001.taskLabelText: In a language class, complete a simple guided writing text about self by filling in blanks. (My name is _____. I am _______. I am from _______. I have _______.) Write answers to simple questions about immediate needs with assistance from a family member or settlement worker.
stl:writing:1:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 1 - Sharing Information

stl:writing:2:getting-things-done:001.description: Fill out the personal identification section of a simple online form to set up an email account. Complete the personal identification sections of an application form for an apartment rental or job benefits. Fill out an application for a newspaper or magazine subscription.
stl:writing:2:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:2:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Fill out the personal identification section of a simple online form to set up an email account. Complete the personal identification sections of an application form for an apartment rental or job benefits. Fill out an application for a newspaper or magazine subscription.
stl:writing:2:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 2 - Getting Things Done

stl:writing:2:interacting-with-others:00.description: Write a note to a neighbour before going on vacation. Include a contact address, timelines, and emergency contact information. Write a short email to invite a friend to lunch. Include details about the time and location. Write a simple message to a friend on a social networking site. Write a short sympathy or get-well message to a friend or co-worker.
stl:writing:2:interacting-with-others:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:2:interacting-with-others:00.taskLabelText: Write a note to a neighbour before going on vacation. Include a contact address, timelines, and emergency contact information. Write a short email to invite a friend to lunch. Include details about the time and location. Write a simple message to a friend on a social networking site. Write a short sympathy or get-well message to a friend or co-worker.
stl:writing:2:interacting-with-others:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 2 - Interacting With Others

stl:writing:2:reproducing-information:00.description: Copy from a dictionary 3 different definitions for the same word to learn the meanings. Copy instructions (such as a short recipe, public transit directions, or instructions for a job application) from a website. Copy a work schedule for personal use. Copy product information to prepare an order for a customer. Copy a child’s school timetable into a day planner for personal use.
stl:writing:2:reproducing-information:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:2:reproducing-information:00.taskLabelText: Copy from a dictionary 3 different definitions for the same word to learn the meanings. Copy product information to prepare an order for a customer. Copy a child’s school timetable into a day planner for personal use.
stl:writing:2:reproducing-information:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 2 - Reproducing Information

stl:writing:2:sharing-information:001.description: Write simple (1 line) descriptions to accompany family photographs that are in an album or online in a photo-sharing application. Write simple (1 line) responses to basic questions from a family member or co-worker in text messages. (I am at work. I am shopping.) In a language class, write a few short personal sentences in response to question prompts.
stl:writing:2:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

In a language class, write a few short personal sentences in response to question prompts.
stl:writing:2:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 2 - Sharing Information

stl:writing:3:getting-things-done:001.description: Fill out an emergency information form for an employer, a school, or a summer camp. Write a short note telling a colleague to turn off the light and lock the door when he/she is leaving. Complete an organ donor card to keep in a wallet.
stl:writing:3:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:3:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Fill out an emergency information form for an employer, a school, or a summer camp. Write a short note telling a colleague to turn off the light and lock the door when he/she is leaving. Complete an organ donor card to keep in a wallet.
stl:writing:3:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 3 - Getting Things Done

stl:writing:3:interacting-with-others:00.description: Write an invitation to a family function, such as a housewarming, graduation, or birthday party. Write a short personal note to thank a host, friend, or supervisor for lunch. Write an email to a friend with a short update on what happened last week.
stl:writing:3:interacting-with-others:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:writing:3:interacting-with-others:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 3 - Interacting With Others

stl:writing:3:reproducing-information:00.description: Copy definitions from 2 or 3 sources (such as online dictionaries or grammar websites). Copy information about 2 products or services from catalogues or online sources to see which has the most features.
stl:writing:3:reproducing-information:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:writing:3:reproducing-information:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 3 - Reproducing Information

stl:writing:3:sharing-information:001.description: Write a short description of a family member. Write a few sentences about a family event or occasion to accompany a picture on a social networking site. Write about a special place. Write about a daily work routine.
stl:writing:3:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.

stl:writing:3:sharing-information:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 3 - Sharing Information

stl:writing:4:getting-things-done:001.description: Fill out an application form for pre-authorized payments for water, power or telephone service. Write an email to an organization to request information or cancel a service. Write a short note to a landlord about a problem in the apartment that needs attention or repair. Write a short, simple paragraph to a supervisor to ask for a day off.
stl:writing:4:getting-things-done:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:4:getting-things-done:001.taskLabelText: Fill out an application form for pre-authorized payments for water, power or telephone service. Write a short, simple paragraph to a supervisor to ask for a day off.
stl:writing:4:getting-things-done:001.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 4 - Getting Things Done

stl:writing:4:interacting-with-others:00.description: Write a formal invitation for a group function (such as a company picnic, BBQ or potluck). Write a letter or email to a friend to describe feelings about a new home town, English class or job. Write a short personal journal to share with a teacher or class.
stl:writing:4:interacting-with-others:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:4:interacting-with-others:00.taskLabelText: Write a formal invitation for a group function (such as a company picnic, BBQ or potluck). stl:writing:4:interacting-with-others:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 4 - Interacting With Others

stl:writing:4:reproducing-information:00.description: Take notes from a pre-recorded telephone message (such as a company message about job openings, a message about a store’s location and hours of operation, or a message detailing a bus or train schedule). Include details for personal use. Take notes from an advertising flyer on products, features, prices and retail locations to inform shopping decisions.
stl:writing:4:reproducing-information:00.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
stl:writing:4:reproducing-information:00.taskLabelText: Take notes from a pre-recorded telephone message (such as a company message about job openings, a message about a store’s location and hours of operation, or a message detailing a bus or train schedule). Include details for personal use. Take notes from an advertising flyer on products, features, prices and retail locations to inform shopping decisions.
stl:writing:4:reproducing-information:00.title: Sample Task - Writing CLB 4 - Reproducing Information

stl:writing:4:sharing-information:001.description: Write a paragraph to describe coming to Canada. Send an email to a co-worker about a trip or vacation. Write to a friend to share information about a new home (house or apartment). Write to a friend, colleague or classmate about plans for next week, next month or next year.
stl:writing:4:sharing-information:001.domainNotes: Canonical sampleTaskLabel text extracted from CLB 2012 benchmark-page fragment.
